name: Keystone Community Development Corporation
description_short: Texas-based 501(c)(3) non-profit offering HUD-certified homebuyer education, down payment assistance grants, and financial counseling to low-to-moderate income families.
description_long:
Keystone Community Development Corporation (KCDC) is a Texas-based 501(c)(3) non-profit organization focused on community economic development and financial education. Founded to serve underserved populations in their community, KCDC operates as a legitimate charitable organization with clear governance under Texas state law and IRS 501(c)(3) status.

KCDC offers five core service areas: HUD-certified homebuyer education classes (designed specifically for first-time homebuyers), down payment and closing cost assistance grants through partnerships with financial institutions and municipalities, financial empowerment counseling under the "Your Money, Your Goals" program, military veteran resource compilation and support, and general financial literacy on topics like money management and debt handling. All programs are delivered by staff who maintain active certifications and participate in ongoing professional development.

The organization distinguishes itself through its dual focus on homeownership access and broader financial empowerment, rather than narrow credit repair or debt settlement. Their down payment assistance program operates through established partnerships with lenders, municipalities, and county programs—indicating institutional relationships rather than direct lending. Staff certification maintenance and conference attendance suggest commitment to quality counseling standards.

A key limitation is geographic scope: the phone number (281 area code) and Texas incorporation suggest primary service area is Texas, likely the Houston region. The website provides limited information about grant amounts, eligibility requirements, application timelines, or program capacity. As a non-profit relying on external funding sources, program availability and grant funding likely fluctuate. No mention of online counseling options or serving clients outside their primary region.
pros: HUD-certified housing counseling agency status—meets federal standards for homebuyer education
501(c)(3) non-profit structure with no profit motive—fees are charitable, not commercial
Down payment assistance grants through institutional partnerships (financial institutions, municipalities, counties)—funded by multiple sources
Military veteran resource focus—specifically compiles and provides targeted support for service members and families
Staff maintains active professional certifications in homebuyer education and financial counseling
Addresses debt and money management holistically through 'Your Money, Your Goals' program—not just credit repair
Free homebuyer education class availability specifically benefiting first-time home buyers
cons: Limited geographic scope—Texas incorporation and 281 area code (Houston region) suggest local or regional service area only
Website lacks concrete information on down payment grant amounts, eligibility criteria, and application process
No clear information about program wait times, grant availability, or annual funding capacity
No mention of online or remote counseling options—may require in-person participation
Grant funding dependent on external sources—program continuity and funding levels not guaranteed year-to-year
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Texas seeking HUD-certified pre-purchase counseling and down payment assistance
Military veterans and their families seeking compiled resources and homeownership support
Low-to-moderate income individuals needing financial literacy and debt management guidance
Texas residents interested in non-profit, mission-driven financial counseling rather than commercial debt services
diagnosis: KCDC is best for Texas residents seeking legitimate, free or low-cost HUD-certified homebuyer counseling and down payment assistance through a non-profit. The main caveat is geographic limitation to Texas (likely Houston area) and lack of detailed information about grant eligibility, amounts, and availability on their website—prospective clients must contact them directly for specific program details.
services: Military veteran resource compilation and support
"Your Money, Your Goals" financial literacy program
Professional financial counseling with certified staff
Partnership-based grant funding through lenders and municipalities
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 309 W X St, Deer Park, TX 77536
